title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: I'm going to celebrate tonight by making a vision board.
I print it off some photos that I'm going to cut and glue onto the board.
While I work on this, I'm going to do my own version of the wrapped trend.
So I'm going to do that and share some of my favorite things from 2025.
I finished cutting, so now I'm going to glue them onto the poster board to make a collage.

title: will / shall
examples: I have a lot of goals for 2026, so this will be a great way to organize them.
It's about a murderer, so it's kind of a creepy and twisted book, but it was very well written and the ending was chef's kiss.
I want to, no, I will be able to do at least one pull-up this year.
I'll tell you, or else it won't come true.

title: will / won't
examples: I'll tell you, or else it won't come true.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: I can't recommend this movie enough.
I want to, no, I will be able to do at least one pull-up this year.
Wow, I can't believe how many people are actually out here this morning.

title: had better / it's time
examples: Now it's time to make a wish.
It's time to hit the gym.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: We got our number, more number two in line, but she said it would take over 30 minutes for us to get our seat.

title: Passive (special: get + p.p.)
examples: I want to volunteer more and get involved in the community.
